macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Job Report Disappoints: Only 57,000 Jobs Added in June, Weakening Labor Market Signals
items.summary: The US economy added just 57,000 jobs in June, barely half the 114,000 expected, while May's numbers were revised down by another 43,000, marking the ugliest job reports seen recently and signaling a weakening labor market.
items.source: YouTube (The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed)

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 3.5% to $62,000 as Fed Rate Hike Odds Collapse from 62% to 47%
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied 3.5% (adding ~$41B to market cap) and reclaimed $62,000 after weaker-than-expected June jobs data lowered the odds of a Fed rate hike on Polymarket from 62% to 47%, while the crypto market cap gained $69B in 6 hours.
items.source: YouTube (The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed)

items.title: Ethereum Up 6.9% and Crypto Liquidations Hit $600M Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Ethereum surged 6.9% (adding $13B to market cap) while over $600M in crypto positions were liquidated, reflecting heightened volatility following the US jobs report and shifting Fed rate expectations.
items.source: YouTube (The Fed Just Blinked and Bitcoin Noticed)

items.title: Gold Hits Major Buying Opportunity as It Surpasses US Treasuries as Top Reserve Asset
items.summary: James Rickards views the recent gold price downturn as a major buying opportunity, projecting gold to hit $10,000 by end of 2026, after gold officially surpassed US Treasuries as the most widely held reserve asset in late 2025.
items.source: YouTube ($10,000 GOLD By Year-End 'Intact' as Crisis 'Worse Than ...')

items.title: Rickards Advises Lightening Stock Exposure: Exit AI, Hyperscalers, Software; Favor Defense and Healthcare
items.summary: James Rickards recommends investors lighten up on stocks, specifically exiting AI, hyperscalers, and software sectors, while favoring defense and healthcare as sectors expected to perform well amid economic uncertainty.
items.source: YouTube ($10,000 GOLD By Year-End 'Intact' as Crisis 'Worse Than ...')

items.title: AI Still Unable to Replace Human Jobs: Expert Clarifies Current Limitations of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Experts emphasize that AI is currently unable to replace human beings or replace jobs, despite ongoing economic crisis discussions and AI being a major topic in current news.
items.source: Instagram (Is Rahul Gandhi clueless about unemployment impact of AI?)

items.title: Companies Shift to Efficient AI Spending After High Token Bills: 'Tokenmaxxing' Out, AI Investment Continues
items.summary: After facing high AI token bills, companies have shifted toward more efficient AI spending strategies, with 'tokenmaxxing' fading but continued investment in AI technologies as Tabs CEO Ali Hussain explains the new outlook.
items.source: Yahoo Finance (Tokenmaxxing is out, but companies are still spending on AI)

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 4% to $62,000 as Fed Rate Hike Odds Collapse
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied 4% to $61,272 following weak US job reports that lowered Polymarket's odds of a Fed rate hike from 62% to 47%, while crypto market cap added over $69 billion in 6 hours.

items.title: Ethereum and Solana Lead Major Price Movements with Sharp Gains
items.summary: Ethereum jumped 6.9% adding $13 billion to its market cap, while Solana confirmed a 3-day candle close above $80, a major bullish signal despite short-term resistance between $81.50 and $83.

items.title: President Trump Pushes 'Clarity Act' as Crypto Becomes Political Priority
items.summary: President Trump declared crypto a 'big deal' and urged Congress to pass the Clarity Act, which Bloomberg Intelligence estimates has a 60% chance of passing this month, marking the final legislative window.

items.title: Trump's $1.2B Crypto Windfall Sparks Ethical Debate and Market Speculation
items.summary: New financial disclosures reveal Trump made over $1.2 billion from crypto in his first year back in office, including $600 million from meme coin sales tied to World Liberty Financial, raising questions about insider advantages.

items.title: Shorts Liquidated $600M as Crypto Market Reclaims Bullish Momentum
items.summary: Over $600 million in short positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours as Bitcoin reclaimed $62,000 and long-term holders absorbed supply amid persistent ETF outflows except for a $263 million inflow yesterday.

items.title: Chainlink Tests Key Resistance with Bullish Divergence Pattern
items.summary: Chainlink is testing a significant resistance zone around $20–$22 (previous support from mid-June) while displaying a massive bullish divergence on the 3-day chart, suggesting potential sideways chop or bullish relief.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic's Latest Claude Models
items.summary: The Trump administration ended a weekslong ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions, including widely available Claude Fable 5 and restricted-access Mythos 5, citing resolved cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: OpenAI Asked to Limit GPT-5.6 Release to Government Partners
items.summary: The Trump administration requested OpenAI restrict the release of its next model, GPT-5.6, to a small set of government-approved partners before wider release due to security concerns.

items.title: Chinese AI Systems Match Anthropic's Mythos in Cybersecurity Scenarios
items.summary: Chinese artificial-intelligence systems have matched the performance of Anthropic's powerful Mythos model in some cybersecurity scenarios, potentially resetting the global tech race and pressuring U.S. AI policy overhaul.

items.title: ChatGPT Remains Dominant Consumer AI with 900M Weekly Users
items.summary: ChatGPT continues as the dominant consumer-facing large language model chatbot with over 900 million weekly active users worldwide, according to IDC Research estimates.

items.title: GLM-5.2 Ranks Among Top 10 Most-Used AI Models
items.summary: GLM-5.2 has ranked as one of the 10 most-used AI models globally, according to OpenRouter data covering access to more than 400 AI models.

items.title: Ford Rehires Engineers Previously Replaced by AI
items.summary: Ford has rehired engineers it previously replaced with artificial intelligence, signaling a corporate shift in AI workforce strategy.

items.title: AI Reveals Ancient Manuscript Secrets and Aids FBI Investigation
items.summary: Recent AI breakthroughs include revealing secrets of an ancient manuscript and assisting the FBI in investigating the shooting at the Correspondents' Dinner.

items.title: Two-Thirds of Workers Secretly Use Banned AI Tools
items.summary: A new report indicates that two-thirds of workers are using banned AI tools in secret, highlighting widespread industry adoption despite regulatory restrictions.

items.title: Microsoft Expands AI Investments and Anthropic explores Samsung 2nm Chips
items.summary: Microsoft is significantly expanding AI investments while Anthropic explores partnerships with Samsung for 2nm chip technology, reflecting major corporate moves in the chip race.

items.title: Elon Musk Suggests Work Could Become Optional Due to Automation
items.summary: Elon Musk has suggested that work could become optional in the future due to rapid automation advancements, a major prediction shaping tech discourse.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Cancellation Rumors Spark Concern as Microsoft Explores Selling Undead Labs
items.summary: Reports indicate State of Decay 3 may be canceled as Microsoft is exploring selling its developer Undead Labs, with multiple studios reportedly on the chopping block including Double Fine and Ninja Theory.

items.title: Obsidian Entertainment Safe After Brief Cancellation Rumors, Xbox Confirms Studio Retention
items.summary: Despite initial reports suggesting Microsoft planned to shut down Obsidian Entertainment, Windows Central and gamebusiness.com have retracted the claims, confirming the studio remains safe and is not working on a new Fallout game.
items.source: Things Get Ugly For Sony & The Star Fox Switch 2 Sales Are In

items.title: Star Fox on Switch 2 Tops Japan Sales Charts with Over 41,000 Copies in Debut
items.summary: Nintendo's new Star Fox game for the Switch 2 has achieved a strong start in Japan, selling 41,680 copies and ranking as the top title in the country's debut sales figures.
items.source: Things Get Ugly For Sony & The Star Fox Switch 2 Sales Are In

items.title: GTA 6 Surpasses $3 Billion in Revenue Within 24 Hours Despite Global Bans
items.summary: GTA 6 has generated an unprecedented $3 billion in just 24 hours, breaking industry records despite facing massive bans in several regions.

items.title: Capcom Announces Onimusha Release Date Shift to September 4, 2026
items.summary: Capcom has moved the release date of Onimusha forward by a few weeks, now set for September 4, 2026, following earlier leaks about the game's early September launch.
items.source: Things Get Ugly For Sony & The Star Fox Switch 2 Sales Are In

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Generate $3 Billion in First 24 Hours
items.summary: GTA 6 achieved a record-breaking $3 billion in revenue within its first 24 hours of pre-orders, selling 39 million units and marking the highest pre-order performance for a video game.

items.title: 1.7 Million Quezin Art Grilling Brushes Recalled Due to Ingestion Hazard
items.summary: Conair announced a voluntary recall of 1.7 million Quezin Art grilling brushes with metal wires after bristles detached and were found in food, leading to three reported cases of bristle ingestion.

items.title: National Mall Introduces Mandatory Security Checkpoints for All Visitors
items.summary: For the first time, all individuals entering the National Mall must pass through security checkpoints equipped with magnetometers, enhancing safety for Fourth of July celebrations.
